## [15:07 - 18:06]

So few people do it in business, but storytellers, novelists, filmmakers, television makers, playwrights, we all understand that surprise is what audiences want and businesses have a million opportunities to surprise people, surprise their customers, surprise their clients, surprise investors. Jobs could have just said, I've got an amazing thing today, it's the iPhone and it's got three things in it, but he doesn't do it, he flips that script. It's just deciding to release information in a different order than what would be expected. That's all surprises, the ordering of information and the providing of clues and misdirection along the way, so that when it happens, it clicks, it's like it was inevitable. in the neurons of your audience, you say it and you put it on the slide at the same time.
